summary: O'Neil argues that the NSF should fund arXiv rather than leaving it to beg for small personal donations. She points out that arXiv plays a pivotal role in basic research across multiple scientific fields, yet operates on less than $1 million per year while the NSF has a $7.5 billion budget. Given the NSF's stated mission to 'promote the progress of science,' she sees the lack of support as inexplicable and outrageous.
key_points: arXiv operates on less than $1 million annually yet is left begging for $5 donations
The platform serves as critical infrastructure for mathematics, physics, computer science, and other fields
The NSF has a $7.5 billion budget and a mission to promote scientific progress
Journal publishing is expensive and time-consuming, making arXiv even more essential
money_quotes: What the fuck is wrong with the NSF? Why isn't it supporting the arXiv?
I have been offended and enraged recently to receive pleading emails from members of the hard-working Cornell University Library arXiv Team for money. As in, please give us $5.
This is a ridiculous state of affairs.
Right now arXiv... plays an absolutely pivotal role in basic research in this country, especially given the expense and time-consuming journal publishing process.
I cannot think of a better way for it to fulfill its mission than to support the maintenance and expansion of the arXiv.
key_insight: Essential scientific infrastructure like arXiv should be publicly funded rather than left to survive on individual donations when federal science agencies have billion-dollar budgets.
